seo.title: Axel Eckenberg — Fullstack & SRE
seo.description: Portfolio of Axel Eckenberg, fullstack engineer and SRE. Co-founder and CTO of EventDeer, focused on Vue, NestJS, Kubernetes and observability.
seo.image_alt: Profile card for Axel Eckenberg, Fullstack and SRE

hero.name: Axel Eckenberg
hero.lede: Engineering student (Epitech, MSc Kent). Co-founder and CTO of EventDeer.
hero.blurb: I build products end to end, from the database to the fibre. Vue, NestJS, Kubernetes, observability. Currently researching efficient interpreters in Rust at Kent.

about.title: Building things that hold up
about.p1: Co-founder and CTO of EventDeer, a Vue/NestJS event management platform for student associations. Live in production, running on bare metal we operate ourselves: Talos on Proxmox, full GitOps with ArgoCD and Kargo, observability through Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Tempo.
about.p2: In parallel I'm finishing an MSc at the University of Kent. Active research project with Michael Vollmer on how Rust's language features shape efficient interpreters (indirect threaded code, dispatch). I'm designing my own minimal language for it rather than starting from an existing codebase.
about.p3: Looking for a backend or platform part-time internship, 3 days a week, starting September 2026. Nantes or remote.

projects.title: Selected work
projects.subtitle: A short list, on purpose. Depth over catalogue.

eventdeer.title: EventDeer
eventdeer.summary: Event management and ticketing platform for student associations.
eventdeer.detail: Vue 3 / Nuxt on the front, NestJS / Postgres on the back. Infrastructure runs on rented OVH bare-metal servers with Proxmox, Kubernetes via Talos, GitOps with ArgoCD and Kargo. Full observability with Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Tempo. External DNS handles OVH records. In production, used by several student associations.

kent.title: Efficient interpreters in Rust
kent.summary: MSc research project on how Rust's language features shape performant interpreters.
kent.detail: Study centred on indirect threaded code and opcode dispatch. Designing a small dedicated language rather than extending an existing codebase, so the impact of Rust's choices (pattern matching, enums, ownership) on branch prediction and real-world performance can be isolated.

lucca.role: Platform & Tooling intern · mentor Brian Guitteny
lucca.title: Lucca
lucca.summary: Internal tooling for product teams (TypeScript, Angular).
lucca.detail: Worked on shared platform tools used across Lucca's product teams under Brian Guitteny's mentorship. TypeScript, Angular, continuous integration. Good school on platform debt, internal libraries, and long-term stability.

freelance.title: Java engagements
freelance.summary: Backend Java work for independent clients.
freelance.detail: A handful of backend Java engagements (Spring), before mostly shifting to the Node / Vue ecosystem.
